content.fact: The late Republic is formally democratic but weakened by corruption, bureaucracy, corporate influence, and Senate paralysis.
content.injection: In late-Republic scenes, treat democracy as still legal and public-facing, but too compromised to respond cleanly to crises.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Late Republic to Imperial Declaration.
Respect story-position bounds from Late Republic Institutional Decay to Galactic Empire Declared.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

content.fact: Before the Clone Wars, the Jedi Order serves the Republic mainly as diplomats, investigators, and peacekeepers rather than as battlefield generals.
content.injection: Before Geonosis, write Jedi authority as institutional and moral, not as military command over clone armies. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

entries.title: Palpatine Uses Naboo To Gain Chancellorship

content.fact: After the Naboo crisis, Palpatine is publicly the Republic Chancellor while secretly Darth Sidious exploiting the crisis he helped create.
content.injection: After Naboo, write Palpatine as publicly lawful and reassuring while secretly steering both political and Sith objectives.

entries.title: Separatist Grievances Are Real But Exploited

content.injection: Write Separatist politics as a mix of sincere anti-Republic grievance, corporate opportunism, and Sith manipulation.
